title: Sales & Production Coordinator – US Client Relations (Neutral Accent Required)
description:
Company: STX Promo | 
 



Location: Remote (Philippines-based) 



Work Hours: Full-Time, U.S. Day Shift (Philippine Overnight) + Light weekend inbox rotation 



Pay Range: $1,100–$1,500/month (Includes premium pay for U.S. hours / Night Shift. Starting rate depends on experience). 



Perks & Benefits: Paid Time Off (PTO), observed US Holidays, end-of-year/performance bonuses (13th-month), and massive room for long-term growth. (Adjust these to fit your exact offerings!)





About STX Promo & Our Growth



STX Promo helps businesses and organizations Step Up Their Setup with eye-catching custom canopies, table covers, flags, banners, and more. We work with small businesses, schools, and nonprofits across the U.S. to elevate their brand presence at events and trade shows.



We are growing incredibly fast. Over the last 10 months alone, we’ve added 4 people to our core team along with several specialized gig workers. As we gear up for our busiest season yet, our current Sales and Production Coordinator is handling a massive volume of orders. We are actively hiring a second full-time coordinator to jump into the trenches, split the workload, and help us maintain our pro-level communication and zero-hassle client experience.





About the Role: Your Impact



We need a highly organized, proactive Sales & Production Coordinator who specializes in Client and Vendor Relations and Process Optimization.



In this role, you are the crucial bridge between our U.S. clients, our internal design team, and our manufacturing suppliers.



Communication Split (80%
 / 20% Phone): While this role is heavily
-based (about 80% of your communication via Gmail and Live Chat), the other 20% will involve speaking directly with our U.S. clients and vendors over the phone. You must be able to confidently handle calls, troubleshoot issues, and provide a friendly, premium customer experience. Because of this, flawless spoken English and a clear, neutral accent are absolute requirements.





Key Responsibilities:



Workflow Optimization: Actively analyze and improve our backend processes using ClickUp, Shopify, and Gmail.



Client Relations (80/20 Split): Manage daily incoming sales and customer service
s/chats, and confidently handle inbound phone inquiries with a friendly, US-facing tone.



Vendor Management: Place precise orders through supplier portals, track delivery times, and troubleshoot delays directly with vendors.



Production Coordination: Collaborate heavily with our Graphic Design team to confirm print graphics and prepare files for handoff.



Workflow Tracking: Organize project priorities using ClickUp. Maintain organized client files within Google Drive.



Schedule & Light Weekend Rotation: Work a consistent Monday–Friday U.S. daytime schedule. You will split a light weekend inbox rotation with our current coordinator (you will not work every weekend) to ensure clients aren't left hanging and deals are closed quickly.





? You're a Great Fit If You Have:



Top-Tier English & Neutral Accent: Extensive experience working directly with U.S.-based clients over voice/phone (US BPO/Call Center experience is a massive plus). You must possess a strong understanding of American business communication and have a clear, neutral accent.



Operational Mindset: You are a problem solver who naturally looks for ways to make systems run smoother.



Vendor/Client Experience: A solid background in managing supplier relationships and keeping clients happy.



Extreme Attention to Detail: You must be hyper-vigilant when approving graphics and placing orders.



Grace Under Pressure: You thrive in a fast-paced environment and keep your cool during the "busy season."



How to Apply (Please Read Carefully):



To ensure your application stands out, you MUST submit the following two items. Applications missing either of these will be immediately deleted.



A Voice Intro (REQUIRED): Please provide a link to a 1-to-2-minute voice recording (you can use a free tool like 
). 



Introduce yourself, highlight your experience dealing with US clients on the phone, and tell us why you are a great fit for this role. We use this to evaluate your spoken English and "phone voice," so please speak naturally and confidently!



Your updated Resume/CV.



We look forward to hearing your intro and finding the next crucial member of our growing team!

title: GoHighLevel expert with Tag Manager exp
description:
Job Title: GoHighLevel (GHL) Expert & Tracking Specialist

Location: US Time Zone (Remote)

Employment Type: Part-time



Job Overview

We are seeking a highly skilled, results-driven GoHighLevel (GHL) Expert who doubles as a Tracking & Analytics Specialist. Your mission is to manage, optimize, and scale our marketing and operations systems within the GHL platform while ensuring every data point is captured accurately via Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics.



The ideal candidate has deep expertise in GHL’s CRM and automation engine and knows exactly how to translate business goals into efficient, automated workflows. You will be the technical owner of our ecosystem, ensuring maximum performance, seamless data tracking, and a frictionless customer journey.



Key Responsibilities



1. Platform Management & Automation



-CRM Configuration: Set up and maintain GHL accounts, including pipelines, custom fields, tags, and user permissions.



-Automation Development: Design and test complex workflows for lead nurturing, healthcare client onboarding, appointment setting, and internal efficiencies.



-Funnel & Website Creation: Build high-converting funnels and landing pages within GHL (Technical build; copy provided).



2. Tracking, Analytics & Data Integrity (The "Must-Haves")



-Google Tag Manager (GTM): Implementation and management of GTM containers across all GHL funnels and sites.



-Google Analytics (GA4): Setup and optimization of GA4 to track conversions, user behavior, and event-based triggers.



-Conversion Tracking: Ensure seamless tracking between GHL forms/surveys and external ad platforms 
/Google Ads).



3. Integrations & Troubleshooting



-Third-Party Tools: Configure and maintain integrations via Zapier/Make, Stripe, and Calendly.



-Technical Support: Act as the primary lead for diagnosing issues with
 deliverability, automation breaks, or tracking discrepancies.



-Reporting: Monitor platform health and generate reports on campaign and pipeline performance to inform our ROI.



Qualifications



-Proven Experience: Minimum of 2+ years of hands-on experience as a GHL Specialist, specifically within the HEALTHCARE sector.



-Tracking Mastery: Expert-level proficiency in Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics is mandatory. You must be able to prove your ability to set up conversion tracking from scratch.



-Technical Depth: Deep knowledge of GHL Workflows, Funnel Builder, CRM, and Calendar configurations.



-Logic-Driven: Strong understanding of conditional logic, custom values, and API integrations.



-Communication: Excellent written and verbal English; ability to explain technical tracking data to non-technical stakeholders.



Preferred (Bonus) Skills



-Experience with GHL SaaS mode and white labeling.



-Familiarity with GHL AI features (AI Phone Agents, Chatbots).



-Basic HTML/CSS for custom landing page or
 tweaks.



-Strong background in Lead Generation strategies.



How to Apply



-If you are a GHL wizard who lives for clean data and automated growth, we want to hear from you.



Send your resume to: 



Subject Line: GHL and TAG MANAGER Expert – [Your Full Name]  please do not forget to attach your work profile.

title: Recruiter VA
description:
Recruiter VA

TOMA Companies

Reports to: Vice President Location: Remote (Philippines) Hours: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM EST, Monday–Friday

Note on hours: This role operates on US Eastern Time. For candidates based in the Philippines, this corresponds to approximately 8:00 PM – 5:00 AM PHT. Please confirm your availability before applying.



About TOMA Companies

TOMA Companies is a growth-focused organization built around a simple but powerful idea: turning properties from nothing into beautiful and useful buildings we are proud of. We operate across four divisions — MinuteMan Car Wash (retail), Car Wash Suppliers (equipment distribution and general contracting), a Real Estate Division (land ownership and leasing), and TOMA Companies as the parent brand. Our niche is land acquisition, development, construction management, and leasing — and we're building toward $100M in annual sales as a regional construction management company.

We run on EOS (Entrepreneurial Operating System), which means every role has clear accountability, measurable outcomes, and a scorecard. We take "right people in right seats" seriously — and that's exactly what this role is responsible for.

Our five core values guide everything we do:

Respect — always be polite

Dedication — committed to what we do

Passion — love what you do for the company

Accountability — owning your role

Ambitious — always improving and adapting

If these values describe how you already operate, keep reading.



About This Role

This is a remote recruiting role that supports all hiring needs across every TOMA Companies division. Your primary volume will come from MinuteMan Car Wash retail staffing, but you'll also recruit for Car Wash Suppliers and other divisions as business demands shift. Workload distribution varies by season and growth — flexibility and range are essential.

Your job is simple in concept: make sure every open seat gets filled with the right person, as fast as possible. You'll own the full top-of-funnel — screening applicants, conducting first-round interviews using a standardized question set, and passing only qualified candidates to management. No hand-holding needed.

Important — We're Building This Together: TOMA Companies is a growing organization and not everything is built out yet. You may be starting without fully established systems, templates, or processes in place. We expect you to take initiative — build your own folder structures, create tracking templates, and propose workflows — then bring them to leadership for approval. If you need everything handed to you before you can get started, this isn't the right fit. If you're energized by building something from the ground up, it absolutely is.



Cross-Team Coordination — Recruiter VA & Ops VA

TOMA Companies uses EOS scorecards to track performance across all roles and divisions. The Ops VA owns the master scorecards and reporting — but the Recruiter VA owns the underlying recruitment metrics that feed into them. This means:

You are responsible for tracking and maintaining your own recruitment metrics: open positions, applicant volume, time-to-fill, screen-to-interview conversion, offer acceptance rates, and source effectiveness — meaning which platforms (Indeed, Glassdoor,
, X, Snapchat, etc.) are generating the most applicants, the most qualified candidates, and the most rejections.

Source tracking is managed through Odoo Recruitment — each role gets a campaign with individual tracked apply links per platform. This allows the team to make data-driven decisions about where to invest in paid job advertising. You are responsible for setting up campaigns correctly and keeping source data clean.

The Ops VA will pull these metrics from you weekly to roll into the company scorecard — you need to keep your data clean, current, and accessible.

You and the Ops VA should communicate proactively. If a position is stuck or a metric looks off, flag it — don't wait for the weekly report.

You will also coordinate with the Marketing VA on employer branding: they own "We're Hiring" social posts and branded recruitment graphics; you own the job board postings and applicant pipeline. Whoever owns the platform owns the post — job boards are yours, social media is theirs.



Key Roles and Accountability

Talent Pipeline: Keep every open position across all TOMA divisions moving — from posting to offer, with no bottlenecks on your end.

Recruitment ROI: Maximize hire quality while minimizing time-to-fill across all business units.

Process Consistency: Follow and maintain the standard recruiting workflow and screening criteria for every role type.

EOS Scorecard: Own your weekly recruitment measurables and ensure the Ops VA has accurate data every week without being asked twice.



Duties

Review hiring stats each morning and flag any applicants or postings that need immediate attention.

Refresh and manage job postings on Indeed, Glassdoor, Ohio Means Jobs, and any other relevant platforms to keep listings active and visible.

Screen new applicants daily and move qualified candidates through the pipeline without delay.

Conduct structured first-round phone screens using a provided question set — assess culture fit, GWC, and basic role qualifications before passing to management.

Evaluate every candidate against TOMA's core values: Respect, Dedication, Passion, Accountability, and Ambition. Culture fit is not a bonus — it's a requirement.

Communicate directly with GMs and department leads to coordinate next steps for qualified candidates.

Follow up with candidates between stages to keep them warm and reduce drop-off.

Track all open positions and candidate statuses in a shared pipeline or tracking sheet; keep data clean for weekly Ops VA reporting.

Communicate daily with the VP on stuck or urgent positions.

Coordinate with team leads across retail, construction/distribution, and real estate divisions to understand staffing needs and set realistic timelines.

Assist with day-one orientations for new remote hires.

Ensure GMs and managers have all necessary paperwork and onboarding materials ready before a new hire's start date.

Continuously improve job post performance by testing different copy, titles, or platforms based on applicant volume and quality — always be improving.



Desired Skills and Experience

Prior experience in recruiting, HR, or talent acquisition — agency or in-house, both welcome.

Fluent English speaker with strong verbal and written communication skills; professional phone presence is a must.

Must have a reliable high-speed internet connection and a backup battery or power solution in case of outages.

Strong question comprehension — able to quickly understand context and respond appropriately without needing things repeated.

Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple open requisitions across different business units simultaneously.

Proficient in job board platforms (Indeed, Glassdoor, Ohio Means Jobs,
, X, Snapchat) and Google Workspace (Sheets, Drive, Docs).

Experience with Odoo Recruitment or similar ATS is a strong plus — you will use Odoo to create job campaigns, generate source-tracked apply links, and report on platform effectiveness. If you haven't used Odoo before, comfort learning it quickly is required.

Self-motivated and able to work independently — dedicated to the work even when no one is watching.

Prior experience working alongside virtual teams or remote-first companies is a plus.

GWC: Must "Get" how the right hires directly impact the success of every wash location and every project, "Want" to fill seats with quality people passionately, and have the "Capacity" — high-speed internet, a backup power solution, and the personal accountability to get it done regardless of obstacles.



Equipment & Technology Requirements

You must provide your own computer or laptop capable of running required software reliably.

You must have a primary high-speed internet connection and a backup internet solution (mobile data, secondary ISP, etc.) in case of outages.

You must have a primary power source and a backup power solution (UPS, generator, or battery backup) to ensure uninterrupted availability during your scheduled hours.

All required software licenses and subscriptions — including Odoo, Google Workspace, and any other paid tools needed to perform this role — will be provided and paid for by TOMA Companies. You will not be expected to purchase software out of pocket.



Compensation and Benefits

$800 USD / month — competitive compensation for a Philippines-based remote role

Annual EOS review with opportunity for a raise based on scorecard performance

Paid training on company-specific tools and processes

Flexible remote environment
